prompts.proposer: Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
prompts.skeptic:

You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["best_single_by_validation", "ridge_stacking_l210_topk5"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set contains ONLY baselines (baseline_mean, robust_median, trimmed_mean_r0.05, trimmed_mean_r0.10). MUST add weighted/selection candidates per guidelines. best_single_by_validation has RMSE=902.96 vs current ~995-998 (significant improvement). n_unique_winners=9≥3 favors per-horizon selection. n_windows=3≤4 requires stronger regularization - ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=0.14<0.25 so DBA not strictly needed but stacking helps with stability.", "changes": ["added best_single_by_validation for high n_unique_winners (9)", "added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for low n_windows regularization"], "when_good": ""}

statistician.rationale: Current candidate set contains ONLY baselines (baseline_mean, robust_median, trimmed_mean_r0.05, trimmed_mean_r0.10). MUST add weighted/selection candidates per guidelines. best_single_by_validation has RMSE=902.96 vs current ~995-998 (significant improvement). n_unique_winners=9≥3 favors per-horizon selection. n_windows=3≤4 requires stronger regularization - ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=0.14<0.25 so DBA not strictly needed but stacking helps with stability.
changes: added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for low n_windows regularization
